concerns.text: Research question asks 'Does standardized mindfulness training... produce measurable changes' (causal language) but design is observational without randomization. FR-009 correctly frames findings as associational, but the fundamental question may be unanswerable by any observational method. Consider reframing to 'Is mindfulness training associated with DMN connectivity changes'.

Constitution Principle VI mandates 'ROI extraction MUST use the AAL atlas coordinates for DMN nodes' but plan.md/research.md use 'Yeo 7-network atlas DMN component'. Different atlases define DMN differently, affecting construct validity. Must align atlas choice across constitution and plan.

No formal power analysis provided. Plan states '<=100 subjects per dataset' and '≥3 datasets' but does not demonstrate this sample can detect expected effect sizes. fMRI connectivity studies typically require n>30 per group for reliable estimates. Post-hoc power analysis (FR-010) is insufficient for planning.

Meta-analysis across ≥3 OpenNeuro datasets introduces unmodeled heterogeneity (different scanners, acquisition protocols, populations). I² > 50% triggers sensitivity analysis but does not address fundamental confounds. Consider modeling scanner/site as random effect or restricting to single-dataset analysis.

Global signal regression included in nuisance regression (research.md:Preprocessing Pipeline) is controversial in fMRI community—can introduce artifactual negative correlations. This methodological choice should be explicitly justified or sensitivity analysis performed with/without GSR.

Plan repeatedly flags 'NO verified source found' for required datasets. While this is a data_resources concern, the methodology cannot proceed without data. Document contingency: if <3 datasets available, single-dataset analysis with explicit generalizability limitations.
